canonical_run.dataset: openFDA FAERS drug-event slice (public domain, no data-use agreement)
canonical_run.label: serious (openFDA `serious` field: death | hospitalization | disability | life-threatening | congenital anomaly)
canonical_run.protocol: leak-safe temporal split; top-K drug/reaction/indication vocabulary ranked on the training window only; LightGBM classifier, raw (uncalibrated) probabilities

reproducer_targets.note: Metrics on the committed 3,700-report openFDA demo slice with the canonical temporal split (seed 42). The demo slice is small and its serious-label prevalence shifts across the split (0.86 train vs 0.71 test), so calibration (Brier/ECE) is loose; the public-method pipeline applies no post-hoc calibrator. Demo numbers are not the full-corpus figures below.

internal_reference.note: The full internal FAERS severity pipeline (multi-quarter corpus, temporal split) reaches the figure below with additional proprietary Rosenbound components (a patented causal-inference layer and an ensemble head) beyond the LightGBM public-method baseline reproduced here. It is recorded for reference and is not reproducible in this repository; the public reproducer is the leak-safe LightGBM baseline only.

faers_full_corpus_lock.method: LightGBM plus a proprietary causal-inference layer, temporal split
